references.title: Fine-Grained Interaction Modeling with Multi-Relational Transformer for Knowledge Tracing

extracted.Students:
8300 ASSISTments 2009 sequences, 
67100 ASSISTments 2012 sequences, 2.3M Ednet-KT1, 447.8k Eedi sequences
extracted.Features: [Sequence of Previous Questions,  Answers and Related Concepts, Sequence of Correctedness]
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models: The current KT models often struggle with non-adjacent interactions between question-answer pairs: DKT implicitly models adjacent interactions (hidden state updates of RNN), and few attention models learn both adjacent and non-adjacent interactions.
extracted.Novelty of Model:
MRT-KT introduces a novel form of relationship encoding - unique for each two question-response pairs.

It also has a multi-relational self-attention mechanism stacked with multi-relational tarrget-dependent attention. 

In addition, the architecture also includes relation-specific temporal kernels.
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Inputs (question, concepts, correctedness) are encoded sequentially to be given as input to the model.

Relationships between 2 question-answer pairs are represented by 2-bit (multi-relational target dependent) and 4-bit (multi-relational self-attention).
extracted.Models: DKT, SKT-Forget, SAKT, EKT-A, AKT, HawkesKT, LPKT, SAINT+, MRT-KT
extracted.Comments:
Authors propose a model  that includes 3 relationship: 

- adjacent past interactions 
- target-dependent interactions between question and past questions, 
- non-adjacent interactions with other questions with distance grater than 1

The proposed model includes 2 attention layers (one for target interactions and the second for non-adjacent past interactions).

Authors do a set of experiments to:

1. Assess model performance against other alterrnatives,
2. Do an ablation study to check the relevance of the different layers introduced in the model.
extracted.Limitations:
- Holdout Method

- No probabilistic modelling, which hinders ability to work with open-ended questions.

annotations.text: Knowledge tracing is an important in student modelling as it is an application that infers knowledge states and allows the prediction of sucess in future questions based on past questions. A limitation of the current knowledge tracing methods is the way question-response pairings are reporesented.

The authors start with an analysis that identifies cross effects between question-respponse pairs within a sequence of anwers. Then, the authors create a multi-relational transformer for knowledge tracing that better encodes question-reponse pairings. Results show that the new Transformer model outperforms state-of-the-art is four widely-used datasets for knowledge tracing.
